items.title: Israel and Lebanon agree to implement ceasefire if Hezbollah stops attacks
items.summary: The countries reject "any attempt, by any state or non-state actor, to hold Lebanon's future hostage", the US says.

items.title: US House delivers rebuke to Trump as it votes to halt Iran war
items.summary: In the 215-208 vote, four Republicans joined Democrats to pass the measure, which is largely symbolic.

items.title: 'Crazy' phone call between Trump and Netanyahu complicates Iran talks
items.summary: Israel's PM laughed off reports of friction, but he has tested the patience of other US presidents.

items.title: Grab what you can while you can: The new reality in the South China Sea
items.summary: After years of watching China create land to back its expansive claims, others are doing the same.

items.title: Missing Sherpa guide found on Mount Everest after 'miracle' self-rescue
items.summary: Cleaners found Dawa Sherpa crawling towards Base Camp six days after he went missing at a higher altitude.

items.title: Ukraine accused of killing four in occupied Crimea
items.summary: At least 10 people have been injured in the attacks, according to Russian-backed officials.

items.title: Heavy gunfire in Somali capital as row over election delay escalates
items.summary: The opposition has called for protests after the president's term in office was extended by a year.

items.title: Trump to nominate Blanche for attorney general on permanent basis
items.summary: If confirmed by the Senate, Trump's former personal lawyer would become the administration's number one prosecutor.

items.title: Indian film union drops boycott call against Bollywood star Ranveer Singh
items.summary: The producers of Don 3 had earlier complained that Singh's alleged exit from the film had caused major financial losses.

items.title: Germany blames Russia for 'bitter defeat' in UN Security Council bid
items.summary: Johann Wadephul said it was "no secret" Russia had stirred up sentiment against Germany over its support for Ukraine.

items.title: China bans four New Zealand MPs over Taiwan visit
items.summary: Upon their return from Taiwan last month, the lawmakers were told that China had banned them for a year.

items.title: Senate Republicans axe $1bn for Trump's new White House ballroom
items.summary:
The bill to fund immigration agencies has been delayed by opposition to Trump's 
'anti-weaponisation fund'.

items.title: Ukrainian drones hit St Petersburg as Putin's flagship economic forum opens
items.summary: Several long-range drones struck oil storage facilities near the city, days before Vladimir Putin is due to address the event.

items.title: One killed and dozens injured in Iranian drone strikes on Kuwait airport
items.summary: Iran says the attack on Kuwait was in retaliation for earlier US strikes on an Iranian oil tanker and island.

items.title: Foreign nationals among at least 21 killed in Delhi fire
items.summary: Many victims were South Asians who had travelled to India for treatment or to accompany relatives.

items.title: Ex-UK political aide and Biden cabinet secretary lead California governor primary vote count
items.summary: Another Democrat, billionaire climate activist Tom Steyer, is in third place as the tally continues.

items.title: Director Wim Wenders withdraws 1975 film over actress's teen topless scene
items.summary: Nastassja Kinski, who appeared in the film when she was 13, said the film-maker "didn't protect me".

items.title: Uneasy neighbours: Can three World Cup hosts put differences aside for a month?
items.summary: The tournament comes after a period of fractious relations between the US, Canada and Mexico.

items.title: Why Canada has generic Ozempic, and the US doesn't
items.summary: Canada is the first G7 country to approve the lower cost weight-loss drugs, which are rolling out in pharmacies this month.

items.title: Costly fuel pushes more Indians to buy electric cars but challenges remain
items.summary: High fuel prices are strengthening the case for EV adoption in the world's third largest auto market.

items.title: The end of Trump's 'anti-weaponisation' fund is another sign Republicans are fighting back
items.summary: The $1.8bn fund would have paid people the Trump administration decided were unjustly and politically targeted.

items.title: From butterflies to breast milk, Uber's list of lost items reveals wild backseat discoveries
items.summary: The rideshare company's annual Lost & Found Index also reveals America's most forgetful city, and the most forgetful day.

items.title: Watch: New footage shows moment Iranian drone struck Kuwait airport
items.summary: One person was killed and more than 60 injured in the Iranian drone strike on Wednesday.

items.title: Black Hawks and robodogs - how one Mexican city is preparing for the World Cup
items.summary: BBC’s Will Grant got a first-hand look at security preparations in Monterrey, as the city gets ready to host four matches.

items.title: Watch: Explosion at fireworks factory in Malta
items.summary: Two men were injured in the explosion and were taken to hospital with minor injuries.

items.title: Watch: Glass doors break as fans scramble to see Pursuit of Jade star
items.summary: Huge crowds formed outside a shopping centre in China to see actor Zhang Linghe.
